Chairman 
100 VALLEY BLVD" ESCONDIDO, 
CALIFORNIA 92025 
(714) 7414608 
The Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Dear Chairman Lowenstein: 
The purpose of this letter is to request an opinion regarding 
the legality of a City Councilman's participation in a govern-
mental decision relating to a client of his employer, as more 
specifically described hereinbelow. 
The radio station, KOWN, is an AM-FM station, owned by Palomar 
Broadcasters, Inc. 
It employs Mr. Douglas Best, Escondido 
City Councilman, as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc 
Jockey. 
Individuals and firms having accounts with KOWN appear 
from time to time before the Escondido City Council on matters 
which entail a vote of the members of said Council. 
The 
factual situation which occasions this request for an opinion 
involves the Dickerson Company, a land development company. 
Said company has a Tentative Subdivision Map relating to a 61 
lot residential subdivision on a 16 acre site in Escondido. 
The City Council was asked by said company to approve the Tenta-
tive Map. 
Shortly before said councilmanic action the Dickerson 
Company had bought time at KOWN and desired that Mr. Best broad-
cast the commercials. 
Mr. Best's employer, KOWN, receives a 
fee for airing the commercial. 
Mr. Best does not receive any 
money directly from the Dickerson account, having declined to 
take the commission resulting from his acquisition of said account. 
Mr. Best describes his remuneration at KOWN as follows: 
"I receive a monthly draw against commissions. 
If I do not sell 
sufficient time to meet the draw I make nothing in excess of 
the draw, except a guarantee I have for doing three newscasts per 
day and my 'Big Band' show every Sunday. 
To date I have not sold 
sufficient time to exceed my draw. 


.. 
Mr. Daniel Lowenstein 
March 28, 1977 
Page Two 
"I have not received, nor will I receive, any compensation 
for the Dickerson account." 
In the first councilmanic action related hereto, i.e. approval 
of the Tentative Map Act, Mr. Best abstained. 
The matter will 
be returning to the City Council for action related to the 
final map. 
Therefore, it is respectfully requested that the 
Commission render an opinion based on the foregoing facts, as 
to the legality of Mr. Best's taking part in the discussions 
and voting on the matter of the Dickerson subdivision map when 
it appears before the Council for the final decision. 
Very truly yours, 
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY 
J. WIEGAND 
City Attorney 
JW:bw

sections.question: Whether a City Councilman employed as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc Jockey at radio station KOWN may participate in governmental decisions relating to a client of his employer (the Dickerson Company, a land developer that has purchased advertising time at KOWN and seeks City Council approval of a tentative subdivision map).
sections.conclusion: Based on the facts presented, assuming the matter will have no material financial effect on the Councilman's interest in KOWN or Palomar Broadcasters, Inc., Councilman Best may participate in City Council discussions and voting on matters related to the Dickerson subdivision map. The FPPC found no financial interest defined under the Political Reform Act that would disqualify him from participation.
sections.question_synthetic: May a city councilman employed by a radio station participate in voting on matters involving companies that are advertising clients of the station, where the councilman has declined direct compensation from the client's account?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the councilman may participate in the voting because he has no direct financial interest in the Dickerson Company decision, receives no compensation from that account, and the matter appears to have no material financial effect on his employment or interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter from City Attorney Wiegand (dated March 28, 1977) and the FPPC response letter from Delbert L. Spurlock (dated March 30, 1977). The text is partially degraded/OCR-corrupted in places, particularly in the conclusion section, but the key legal analysis and conclusion are discernible. Confidence reduced due to document quality and fragmented text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a City Councilman employed as an Account Executive, Newscaster and Disc Jockey at radio station KOWN may participate in governmental decisions relating to a client of his employer (the Dickerson Company, a land developer that has purchased advertising time at KOWN and seeks City Council approval of a tentative subdivision map).

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts presented, assuming the matter will have no material financial effect on the Councilman's interest in KOWN or Palomar Broadcasters, Inc., Councilman Best may participate in City Council discussions and voting on matters related to the Dickerson subdivision map. The FPPC found no financial interest defined under the Political Reform Act that would disqualify him from participation.

embedding.summary: The FPPC issued advice that a city councilman employed by a radio station may vote on matters involving the station's advertising clients, where the councilman declines direct compensation from those clients and has no other financial interest triggered by the decision.
